frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Vikram: Yaar, honestly you look the most graceful in saree or even a simple salwar, bilkul decent and elegant. These western party-type clothes are fine and all, but Mumbai mein log judge bhi karte hain and stare bhi, so thoda apni culture wali dressing better lagti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames traditional clothing as more 'decent' and links western clothes to being judged or stared at, subtly shifting responsibility for harassment onto women's dress. It encodes the surface manifestations of backhanded compliment, decency framing, and the clothing-harassment link.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
